3.why: We need the size to wire the chain.
3.invariant: n is the number of nodes.

4.why: nextp[i] holds the id node i points to.

5.happening: Loop over every node to wire the original chain.
5.why: Each node links to the next in order.

6.happening: Node {i} is not the tail — point it forward.
6.why: Interior nodes link to the next.
6.invariant: Only the tail ends the list.

6.why: The last node points at nothing.

10.why: It flashes whichever link we just rewired.
10.invariant: changed holds the last rewired node id.

11.why: slow moves one node at a time.

12.why: fast moves two nodes at a time.

13.happening: fast can still take two hops — keep finding the middle.
13.why: When fast reaches the end, slow sits at the middle.

13.happening: fast can't hop twice more — slow is now at the middle.
13.why: The list is split here into two halves.
13.invariant: slow marks the end of the first half.

14.why: The tortoise moves at half speed.

15.why: The hare moves twice as fast.
15.invariant: When fast ends, slow is the middle.

16.happening: second = the node just after the middle ({second}).
16.why: It's the head of the second half we'll reverse.

17.happening: There is a second half — split the list after the middle.
17.why: We reverse the second half separately.
17.invariant: The first half will end at slow.

17.happening: No second half (list too short) — nothing to reverse.

18.happening: Cut the list after the middle (node {slow}'s next becomes none).
18.why: This separates the first and second halves.
18.invariant: The first half now ends at slow.

19.why: Highlight where the list was cut.

20.happening: Seed the reversal with an empty tail (prev = none).
20.why: The reversed half will build up behind prev.
20.invariant: prev is the head of the reversed part so far.

21.happening: Start the reversal walk at second.
21.why: We flip each link of the second half.
21.invariant: curr is the node being reversed.

22.happening: Still nodes in the second half — reverse the next link.
22.why: Each node's next is flipped to point backward.
22.invariant: Everything before curr is already reversed.

22.happening: The second half is fully reversed.
22.why: curr has run off the end.
22.invariant: prev is the head of the reversed second half.

23.happening: Remember node {curr}'s next before overwriting it.
23.why: We must not lose the rest of the half.

24.happening: Flip node {curr}'s next to point backward at prev.
24.invariant: node {curr} now points at the previously-reversed part.

26.why: curr is the new head of the reversed part.

27.happening: Move curr on to the saved next node.
27.why: Continue reversing along the original half.
27.invariant: curr walks the remaining second half.

28.happening: Start the merge's first pointer at the head.
28.why: We weave from the front half.

29.happening: Start the merge's second pointer at the reversed head ({second}).
29.why: We weave in nodes from the reversed second half.

30.happening: The reversed half still has nodes — weave the next one in.
30.why: We alternate a front node then a back node.
30.invariant: The reordered prefix ends at first.

30.happening: The reversed half is exhausted — the merge is done.
30.why: Every back node has been interleaved.

31.why: We rewire first's next, so save it first.
31.invariant: temp1 keeps the front half's remainder.

32.why: We rewire second's next, so save it first.
32.invariant: temp2 keeps the reversed half's remainder.

33.why: Interleave a back node right after the front node.

35.happening: Link node {second}'s next to the saved front continuation.
35.why: Reconnect so the front half continues after the woven node.
35.invariant: node {second} now precedes the next front node.

36.happening: Advance first to the saved front continuation.
36.why: Move to the next front node.

37.happening: Advance second to the saved back continuation.
37.why: Move to the next reversed node.

38.happening: Start an empty list to read out the result.
38.why: We traverse the rewired chain to produce the answer.
38.invariant: order will hold the reordered values.

39.happening: Begin the read-out at the head.
39.why: The reordered list starts at node 0.

40.happening: Still nodes to read — continue the traversal.
40.why: We collect every value in the new order.
40.invariant: order holds the values read so far.

40.happening: Reached the end of the reordered list.
40.why: cur has fallen off the end.
40.invariant: order is the full reordered sequence.

41.why: It's the next value in the reordered list.

42.why: Move to the next node in the new order.
42.invariant: cur advances along the reordered chain.

43.why: Find-middle, reverse, and merge are complete.

init.happening: Wiring the original chain and placing slow and fast at the head.
init.why: We need the chain before finding the middle.
init.invariant: slow and fast both start at the head.

loop.happening: Finding the middle, reversing the second half, or merging — or reading out.
loop.why: The algorithm runs in three phases then a traversal.
loop.invariant: Each phase preserves the nodes, only relinking them.

check.happening: Deciding whether a second half exists to reverse.
check.why: A too-short list needs no reordering.
check.invariant: Only lists of 2+ nodes are split.

move.happening: Advancing a pointer — slow/fast, the reversal cursor, or the merge cursors.
move.why: Each phase walks its pointers along the chain.

update.happening: Rewiring a next pointer (and flashing it) or saving a continuation.
update.why: Splitting, reversing, and merging are all link rewrites.

return.why: All three phases and the read-out are done.
